_**User (2026-01-22 06:04:25Z)**_

We need to fix the bullet creation the same way we did in the past on the Station Notepad. Please figure out how we fixed it in the past and implement that fix once again. here is soem context:

. This sets `_lineStyles[lineIndex] = LineStyle.Bullet`

But if `lineIndex` is wrong (pointing to the previous line), then the heading gets converted to bullet. The real problem might be that when you press Enter at the end of a line before typing anything, the new line might not exist yet in the `_lineStyles` array, so `CurrentLineIndex` could be returning an incorrect index or the array bounds are off.

When the user presses Enter, `OnTextChanged` fires with the updated text, lines get split, and `_lineStyles` extends to match. The new line should initialize as `LineStyle.Normal`. However, when clicking the bullet button, `CurrentLineIndex` counts newlines in `_inputField.text` up to the caret position—but the text contains rich text tags while `caretPosition` is the visual position. If the heading has formatting tags like `<size=150%><b>Test</b></size>`, those tags add character count that doesn't correspond to visual characters, causing a mismatch between the calculated line index and the actual line.

The root issue is that `caretPosition` represents a visual position in the rendered text, but we're using it to index into the raw text with markup. I should either strip the tags before calculating or leverage TMP's `textInfo` to properly map visual positions to line numbers. Using `textInfo` seems more reliable than manually parsing the raw text.The issue is that `_inputField.text` contains rich text tags but `caretPosition` is the visual position. So counting newlines in the tagged text gives wrong results. Let me fix this: The fix now uses `_inputField.textComponent.GetParsedText()` which returns the **actual displayed text without rich text tags**. This correctly maps the visual caret position to the right line.

The bug was: `_inputField.text` contains `<size=150%><b>Heading</b></size>\n` (many characters for tags), but `caretPosition` is based on visual position `Heading\n` (just 8 characters). Counting newlines in the wrong text gave wrong line indices.

Please restart the game and test.

---

  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nPosition caret after the prefix for bullet style\r\n            if (style == LineStyle.Bullet)\r\n            {\r\n                PositionCaretAfterPrefix(lineIndex);\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n        /// Position the caret after the line's prefix (e.g., bullet character).\r\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n        private void PositionCaretAfterPrefix(int lineIndex)\r\n        {\r\n            if (_inputField == null || _inputField.textComponent == null) return;\r\n            \r\n            // Force update to get accurate parsed text\r\n            _inputField.textComponent.ForceMeshUpdate();\r\n            string parsedText = _inputField.textComponent.GetParsedText();\r\n            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(parsedText)) return;\r\n            \r\n            // Find the start of the target line in the parsed text\r\n            string[] parsedLines = parsedText.Split('\\n');\r\n            int charPos = 0;\r\n            \r\n            for (int i = 0; i &lt; lineIndex &amp;&amp; i &lt; parsedLines.Length; i++)\r\n            {\r\n                charPos += parsedLines[i].Length + 1; // +1 for newline\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            // Now add the length of the bullet prefix (\"• \" = 2 chars)\r\n            if (lineIndex &lt; parsedLines.Length &amp;&amp; parsedLines[lineIndex].StartsWith(\"• \"))\r\n            {\r\n                charPos += 2; // Skip past \"• \"\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            // Set caret position\r\n            _inputField.caretPosition = charPos;\r\n            _inputField.selectionAnchorPosition = charPos;\r\n            _inputField.selectionFocusPosition = charPos;\r\n            \r\n            // Ensure input field stays focused\r\n            _inputField.ActivateInputField()\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",

The user is saying:
1. When they click the bullet button, a bullet appears correctly
2. But the caret is positioned to the LEFT of the bullet
3. When they start typing, it adds a second bullet

The issue is that after applying the bullet style, the caret needs to be positioned AFTER the bullet prefix in the rendered text, not at the beginning of the line.

Let me look at the ApplyStyleToLine method and see how to fix the caret positioning.The caret needs to be positioned AFTER the bullet prefix when the style is applied. Let me fix the `ApplyStyleToLine` method: I see there's a `PositionCaretAfterPrefix` method but it's not being called. The issue is that after RefreshDisplay, the caret position needs to be adjusted to account for the newly added bullet prefix in the rendered text. Let me fix this: Build succeeded. The fix now:
1. After applying bullet style, calls `PositionCaretAfterPrefix()`
2. That method uses the **parsed text** (rendered output without tags) to find the correct position
3. Skips past the `"• "` prefix (2 characters) so typing starts after the bullet

Please restart the game and test clicking the bullet button on a line - the caret should now appear after the bullet, not before it.

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is saying:
1. They get an error when pressing F5
2. There's no "Stationpedia Ascended" section in the controls menu

This suggests the Harmony patch on `KeyManager.SetupKeyBindings` isn't working, or it's running at the wrong time. Let me think about this:

1. The patch might not be applied before the game's Settings menu is loaded
2. `KeyManager.GetKey("ToggleStationpedia")` returns `KeyCode.None` if the key isn't registered, which would cause issues
3. The `SetupKeyBindings` might be called BEFORE our Harmony patches are applied

The issue is likely timing - `KeyManager.SetupKeyBindings()` is called during game initialization from `Settings.LoadSettings()`, which probably happens before our mod's `ApplyHarmonyPatches()` is called.

Let me check when SetupKeyBindings is called and think about alternative approaches:

1. Option A: Call our keybinding registration manually in Awake/Start after the patch is applied (since SetupKeyBindings already ran)
2. Option B: Use a coroutine to wait for KeyManager to be ready, then add the keybinding directly

The best approach is probably to:
1. Keep the Harmony patch for future game starts where our patch might be in place first
2. Also manually call the registration code in our Initialize() method to handle the case where SetupKeyBindings already ran

Let me also handle the case where KeyManager.GetKey returns KeyCode.None - we should fall back to F5 in that case.

I need to:
1. Add a fallback in the StationNotepadHotkey property for when the key isn't registered yet
2. Call the keybinding registration directly during Initialize() (not just rely on the Harmony patch)

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Now I can see the issue clearly! The error is:

```
Save Failed: Folder name is empty.
at XmlSaveLoad.QuickSaveCurrentWorld ()
```

And looking at the stack trace, it's triggered by:
```
at InputSystem.KeyWrapBindings.KeyWrapOnEvent (InputSystem.InputContext context)
```

This means F5 is bound to the game's QuickSave function! When the user presses F5, it's triggering the game's quicksave, not our Station Notepad. The "Folder name is empty" error happens because they're in the main menu (no save loaded).

The problem is:
1. F5 is already bound to QuickSave in the game
2. Our keybinding registration code isn't showing any log output (no "Registered custom keybinding" message in the log)
3. The Station Notepad isn't being toggled at all

Two issues to fix:
1. Our custom keybinding registration isn't working (no log message shows)
2. Even if it did work, we need to use a different default key since F5 is QuickSave

Let me check what F5 is bound to in the game, and also fix our registration to actually work. The issue might be that KeyManager.KeyItemLookup or AllKeys might be null when we try to access them, or there's an exception being swallowed.

Also, I notice the user said they don't see our keybinding in the settings menu - which confirms our registration isn't working.

Let me look at this more carefully. The log shows:
- `[Stationpedia Ascended] v0.3.0 initialized successfully!` 

But NO message about keybinding registration. That means either:
1. `RegisterCustomKeybinding()` isn't being called
2. It's throwing an exception before the log message
3. The "already registered" check is passing (unlikely)

Let me check if Initialize() is calling RegisterCustomKeybinding()...


I need to verify the RegisterCustomKeybinding() call is actually in the Initialize() method. The core issue is that F5 is already mapped to QuickSave, so I should switch the default to F8 instead. I'll add more detailed error logging to the registration method to catch any exceptions that might be silently failing, and ensure the method is being invoked during initialization.
